dtcs.description: Mahindra — Smart Door Handle / push-button start fault
Mahindra BCM — Battery voltage low
Mahindra — Brake-pedal switch circuit (cruise interlock)
Mahindra — Air-suspension compressor fault (XUV700 AX7L)
Mahindra — Air-suspension reservoir pressure too low
Mahindra mHawk diesel — Fuel rail pressure too low
Mahindra mHawk diesel — Fuel rail pressure too high
Mahindra — Low-pressure fuel system pressure too low
Mahindra mHawk — Fuel system leak (large)
Mahindra mStallion T-GDi — Turbocharger overboost
Turbocharger Underboost (mHawk 2.2 / mStallion 2.0)
repair_guidance: 1) Confirm boost target vs actual via live data.
2) Smoke-test the intake / charge-air system for leaks.
3) Inspect wastegate solenoid and actuator rod travel.
4) Check for cracked CAC, intercooler, or hose clamps.
5) Replace failed solenoid / actuator if signal correct.

dtcs.description: Mahindra — CKP Crankshaft Position Sensor 'A' Circuit
Mahindra — Camshaft phasing performance (mStallion)
Mahindra mHawk — EGR insufficient flow
Mahindra — Secondary air injection incorrect flow
Catalyst Bank 1 efficiency below threshold (mStallion)
Mahindra — EVAP large leak (loose fuel cap)
Mahindra Aisin AT — Torque converter clutch performance
Mahindra BE EV — HV battery isolation fault
Mahindra mHawk BS-VI — DPF efficiency below threshold
DPF Differential Pressure Too Low (mHawk Diesel)
repair_guidance: 1) Read freeze-frame data and verify the fault is current.
2) Inspect the affected sensor / actuator wiring for chafing, corrosion, or loose connectors.
3) Measure circuit resistance + supply voltage with a multimeter.
4) Compare live data against expected values per OEM service data.
5) Replace the defective component if all wiring tests pass.
